title: cordia obliqua var. tomentosa
intro: Cordia obliqua var. tomentosa, also known as the 'Pitaya Cordia' or 'Wild Cherry', is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its attractive white flowers and edible fruit. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Cordia obliqua var. tomentosa include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to help manage diabetes and hypertension. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its medicinal benefits.
